Q7 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Read the passage carefully and answer the following question.
Among Nature’s most intriguing phenomena are the partnerships formed by many different species. The name used for these relationships, Symbiosis, comes from Greek meaning “living together”. Not all symbiotic relationships are the same. There are some called commensal relationships, in which one partner gains a benefit while the other gains little or none but is not
harmed. One example is the relationship between two types of fish – remoras and sharks. The remora, which is long and often striped, attaches itself to a shark (sometimes to another type of fish or a whale), using a sucker on its head. When the shark makes a kill, the hitchhiker briefly detaches itself to feed on the scraps. Another type of symbiotic relationship is parasitism, in which one partner benefits at the expense of others. Ticks and tapeworms are among familiar parasites.
The third type of symbiotic relationship, called mutualism, is a true partnership in which both partners benefit. The relationship may be limited as when zebras and wildebeest graze together on the vast African grasslands. Each species can survive on its own, but together their chances of detecting predators are improved because each contributes a specially keen sense. (Zebras have
the better eyesight; wildebeest, hearing and sense of smell). In a few cases, partners are so interdependent that one cannot survive without the other. Most mutualistic relationships probably lie somewhere in between.
The passage talks about how animals

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The passage talks about symbiosis i.e. how animals live together.

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : The cost function is convex.
S2 : Gradient descent may converge to local minima.
S3 : Squared error penalizes large deviations more heavily.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• The cost is a quadratic function in θ. Quadratic functions are convex (bowl-shaped). So, S1 is true.
• Since the function is convex, it has a unique global minimum. Gradient descent cannot get stuck in local minima. So, S2 is false.
• Because error is squared, larger errors grow quadratically and are penalized more strongly. So, S3 is true.

For the Marks table, the column R_no and Subject together for the composite key and R_no is the
foreign key referencing to R_no. in Student table.
Select Sum(M.marks), S.S_name
From Student S, Marks M
where M.mark > 70
Group By S.S_name;
The number of rows returned by the above SQL query is ________.
NAT
Correct answer: 5
Solution

5
Condition applied on Mark in Marks table.
Group by Sname, in final table so remain with 5 unique S_name tuples.

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about model errors:
S1 : Increasing the number of features in a model always decreases bias.
S2 : Increasing the number of features in a model may increase variance.
Which of the above is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Adding more features can reduce bias if they are relevant, but “always” is too strong. Irrelevant or noisy features may not reduce bias. So, S1 is incorrect.
• More features increase model complexity, making it more sensitive to training data, which can increase variance. So, S2 is correct.

S2 : Given a weighted directed graph G = (V, E) containing a negative weight cycle, running the Bellman-Ford algorithm from S will find a shortest acyclic path from s to a given destination vertex t.

Q30 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Dimension tables in a star schema are usually denormalized.
S2 : Star schema design reduces query complexity for OLAP operations.
S3 : Fact tables contain textual descriptive information about business entities.
Which is above statements is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Dimension tables are denormalized to reduce joins and simplify queries, improving OLAP performance. So, S1 is correct.
• Star schema design reduces query complexity because the central fact table connects directly to denormalized dimension tables. So, S2 is correct.
• Fact tables store numeric measures like sales or revenue, not textual descriptive information, which is stored in dimension tables. So, S3 is incorrect.

Q38 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is true about training a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) using backpropagation?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a)
• Backpropagation applies the chain rule efficiently by reusing partial derivatives of intermediate
layers (similar to dynamic programming). So, option (a) is correct.
• Training an MLP involves a non-convex error surface, so backpropagation does not guarantee
convergence to the global minimum. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Backpropagation requires differentiable (or sub-differentiable, like ReLU) activation functions.
Non-differentiable functions cannot be trained. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Backpropagation updates all layers, not just the last one, since error gradients are propagated
backward. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q50 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider admissible heuristics and A* search:
(a) A* with an admissible and consistent (monotone) heuristic is optimal and complete when branching factor is finite and step costs are bounded below by a positive constant.
(b) A* with an admissible heuristic but unbounded branching factor is always complete.
(c) If step costs can be arbitrarily small positive numbers (approaching zero), A* may fail to be complete even with admissible h.
(d) Greedy best-first search (using only heuristic value) is guaranteed complete if the heuristic is admissible.
Which of the following statements are correct?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• If the heuristic is admissible and consistent, A* will not overestimate cost and will expand
nodes in non-decreasing f = g + h. With finite branching and a positive minimum step cost, the
search cannot generate infinitely many cheaper nodes, so A* is both complete and optimal.
So, option (a) is correct.
• With an unbounded branching factor, a node may generate infinitely many successors. Even
with admissible h, A* might never reach the goal because it keeps expanding new nodes.
So completeness is not guaranteed. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• If step costs can be arbitrarily small (close to zero), A* may keep expanding infinite paths of
very low cost before ever reaching the goal. Without a positive cost bound, completeness can
fail. So, option (c) is correct.
• Greedy best-first search chooses nodes by h(n) only. Even if h is admissible, the algorithm
may loop or miss the goal in infinite spaces. Thus it is not complete in general. So, option (d) is
incorrect.

S2: If a matrix p with dimensions m by n has a pivot position in every row, then the equation
px = b has a unique solution for every b in the set of real numbers of dimension m.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Q52 NAT Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
I : DFS has lower space complexity than UCS.
II : UCS guarantee can optimal solution even if stap cost.
III : UCS takes more space complexity than BFS.
How many number of the above statement is correct?

NAT
Correct answer: 2
Solution

2
I : DFS stores only a single path and its siblings, giving space complexity Ο(b ⋅ m) vs. UCS’s
exponential memory usage. So, I is correct.
II : UCS expands nodes in order of increasing path cost, ensuring the first goal found is optimal.
So, II is correct.
III : UCS stores all frontier nodes, similar to BFS, but space depends on the cost distribution; in
some cases, BFS may use more memory if depth is large. So, III is incorrect.

Q53 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
(a) False: A min-heap can provide next largest element, we need to do a linear search through the
heap’s array, it will take Ο(n) time.
(b) True: Find the next smallest element, the predecessor, may require travelling down the height
of the tree, making the running time Ο(n) in worst case.
(c) True: Binary heap to BST conversion take Ο(n log n) time.
(d) True: By heapify method.

Q55 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
• After splitting, subsets become more homogeneous, reducing disorder. Hence entropy generally decreases. So, option (a) is correct.
• Entropy does not increase after splitting, otherwise the split would make the tree less informative. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• The goal of decision tree learning is to choose splits that maximize information gain, which means highest reduction in entropy. So, option (c) is correct.
• Information gain is mathematically defined as the entropy of the parent minus the weighted sum of entropies of its children. So, option (d) is correct.
